data.narrative:
Near Space Labs is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, including Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, implying a valuation of $76M. Peer-median anchoring across three same-sector, same-stage companies yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the Space & Aerospace sector median P/S ratio is 42.9x across the five public comparables, Near Space Labs' revenue is not on file.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $65M to $1.5B, with a median of $688M. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between early-stage funding history and peer benchmarking: stage-progression anchors lower, while peer multiples—dominated by Cowboy Space at $2.0B—pull substantially higher. Key uncertainties include whether Near Space Labs' business trajectory aligns with the higher-valuation outliers in the peer set and whether unrevealed revenue or unit economics would materially narrow the range via P/S or revenue multiple approaches.
